title: Explore capital Algerian
article:
Nicknamed "White City" for its enchanting white buildings overlooking the Mediterranean Sea, Algiers is a city where modernity blends seamlessly with the rich history of the Ottoman and French empires.

🇩🇿 Algiers, the capital of Algeria, is a pearl of the Mediterranean and a city of civilization.

Algiers is the country's premier tourist destination, offering visitors a unique experience that combines historical, cultural, and leisure tourism.

1. The Casbah (Old City)

A visit to the capital is incomplete without exploring the alleyways of the Casbah, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

Description: A labyrinth of narrow streets, traditional whitewashed houses, and historic mosques.

Key landmarks include the Rais Palace (Fort 23) and the Ketchaoua Mosque, an architectural masterpiece that blends Ottoman and Moorish styles.

2. The Martyrs' Memorial (Riad El Feth)

The most famous symbol of Algiers, visible from all parts of the city. Significance: Designed in the shape of three palm leaves, symbolizing the three renaissances (industrial, agricultural, and cultural).

Activities: You can visit the "Mujahid Museum" located below the monument and stroll through the Riadh El Feth complex, which includes shops and restaurants.

3. Jardin d'Essai (Hamma)

Known as the "lungs of the capital," it is considered one of the most beautiful botanical gardens in the world.

Features: It contains more than 2,500 species of plants and perennial trees.

Atmosphere: Divided into a French-style garden and an English-style garden, some scenes from the famous film "Tarzan" were filmed here.

4. Notre Dame d'Afrique (Our Lady of Africa) Church

Located on a plateau overlooking the Bay of Algiers, it is a masterpiece of Byzantine architecture.

Why visit?: To enjoy an unforgettable panoramic view of the sea and the city at sunset.

5. The Great Mosque of Algiers

It is the third largest mosque in the world after the two Holy Mosques in Mecca and Medina, and the largest mosque in Africa.

Features: It boasts the tallest minaret in the world and houses a cultural center, a vast library, and a museum of Islamic art.

6. The Waterfront and Didouche Mourad Street

To experience the atmosphere of the modern city:

The Waterfront: It closely resembles the streets of Paris with its grand colonial buildings.

Didouche Mourad Street: This is the bustling commercial artery where you will find the best cafes, traditional bakeries, and shops.
